utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes entries for all five of these items. Now check if each is fully specified with all details: The format expects each coverage to be specified and include costs. For health_coverage: provided plan name and cost per month (has costs) – sufficient. Property_coverage: plan name and cost per month – sufficient. Travel_coverage: plan name and cost per month – sufficient. Additional_services: plan name and cost per month – sufficient. Total_budget: monthly and annual cost breakdown – sufficient. Therefore all 5 required items are present and include costs/details as per the format. Note: There's an extra professional_liability_coverage item that isn't required; it doesn’t affect the count.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to map each plan in the final coverage plan to its corresponding rating from the ground-truth. The items explicitly chosen are: HealthFirst Standard Plan (Physical & Mental) — appears under both primary health physical and mental health sections with ratings 7 and 6 respectively, but the final plan lists a single combined plan name; we should map to the HealthFirst Standard Plan ratings as they appear. For property, HomeFirst Standard Coverage is listed and exists with rating 9; the Technology Protection Add-on corresponds to Technology Protection with rating 8. For travel, TravelFirst Standard Plan corresponds to rating 9; the Adventure Travel Coverage add-on corresponds to Adventure Travel Coverage with rating 10. For additional services, Premium Insurance Membership corresponds to rating 6. The professional liability entry is not a specific product from the ratings and thus has no rating to map.
